{"id":"smallhold-oiu","title":"AppError: structured error type with HTTP mapping","description":"Define the AppError enum that all handler functions return. Must map cleanly to HTTP status codes and produce Mastodon-compatible error response bodies: {"error": "message"}. All error variants carry enough context to log usefully without leaking internals to the client.

Agent workflow: Spawn a subagent to catalog all error shapes in the Mastodon API (from mastodon.social API docs and live testing). Error shapes vary by endpoint — 404 on unknown resource, 422 on validation, 401 on bad token.

Test discipline:
- Test each error variant serializes to correct HTTP status + JSON body
- Test that error bodies never contain internal details (no stack traces, no SQL errors, no file paths)
- Use snapshot testing (insta crate or similar) to lock error response shapes

Defensive programming:
- sqlx::Error::Database with SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE maps to 422 with 'already exists' message, NOT 500
- sqlx::Error::Database with SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGN_KEY maps to 422 with 'referenced resource not found'
- All other sqlx errors map to 500 with generic message, log full error at ERROR level
- activitypub_federation errors: log at WARN, return appropriate HTTP status
- reqwest timeout: log at WARN, return 504 if relevant to a client request","design":"Use thiserror for derive. Implement axum::response::IntoResponse for AppError. Implement From<sqlx::Error> with careful variant matching.","acceptance_criteria":"Every handler returns Result<impl IntoResponse, AppError>; no handler has a bare .unwrap() or .expect(); 422 on constraint violations; 401 on auth failures; error body is always {"error": "..."}","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:26:22Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:26:22Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-4vg","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:34Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
